title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is concentrated in governance and evaluation infrastructure rather than new model capabilities. The most consequential updates tighten pre-deployment testing for frontier systems, add formal safety documentation for a major model release, and push on transparency standards for synthetic media, while a smaller set of research papers advances interpretability and trustworthy evaluation in high-stakes domains.

items.title: NIST’s CAISI expands frontier AI national security testing agreements with Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI
items.why_it_matters: It strengthens pre-deployment government access to frontier model testing and preparedness research.

items.title: OpenAI publishes GPT-5.5 Instant system card
items.why_it_matters: System cards are primary safety disclosures on model behavior, evaluations, and deployed safeguards.

items.title: Partnership on AI outlines AI-generated media transparency priorities for the EU Code of Practice
items.why_it_matters: It informs emerging rules for provenance, disclosure, and accountability around synthetic media.

items.title: Mechanistic interpretability paper argues superposition is not necessary in transformer time-series representations
items.why_it_matters: It extends interpretability methods beyond language models and probes when transformer features stay disentangled.

items.title: MRI-Eval introduces a tiered benchmark for LLM knowledge of MRI physics and scanner operations
items.why_it_matters: It targets domain-specific evaluation where model errors could affect high-stakes clinical workflows.

items.title: Trustworthy mental health prediction framework emphasizes evidential reasoning and uncertainty
items.why_it_matters: It addresses overconfidence and reliability concerns in sensitive mental health inference systems.

items.title: Medical imaging paper proposes VLM-based distributional OOD detection with localization
items.why_it_matters: Better out-of-distribution detection can reduce silent failures in clinical vision-language deployments.

items.title: LoViF 2026 PhyScore challenge focuses on holistic evaluation of 4D world-model outputs
items.why_it_matters: It pushes evaluation beyond visual quality toward physical consistency and world-model reliability.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
